resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def numberOfSubstrings(self, s: str) -> int:
        count = 0
        left = 0
        freq = [0, 0, 0]
        for right in range(len(s)):
            freq[ord(s[right]) - ord('a')] += 1
            while all(freq):
                count += len(s) - right
                freq[ord(s[left]) - ord('a')] -= 1
                left += 1
        return count
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sliding-window technique counts valid substrings ending at each right index. Once the window contains at least one of each character, every extension to the right is valid, so add len(s)-right to the answer and shrink the left boundary until the window no longer satisfies the condition.
